name: python-codelens-lite
description: Lightweight, configurable CodeLens for Python (references, implementations, scoped imports).

pythonCodeLensLite.enable.description: Enable or disable all CodeLens features provided by this extension.

pythonCodeLensLite.targets.methods.description: Show CodeLens for methods (functions inside classes).

pythonCodeLensLite.targets.moduleVariabl.description: Show CodeLens for top-level variables and constants.

pythonCodeLensLite.targets.imports.proje.description: Show CodeLens for imported modules that reside within the current workspace.

pythonCodeLensLite.targets.imports.venv.description: Show CodeLens for modules imported from virtual environments (site-packages/dist-packages).

pythonCodeLensLite.targets.imports.stdli.description: Show CodeLens for standard library imports.

pythonCodeLensLite.targets.imports.globa.description: Show CodeLens for imports that cannot be securely classified into project, venv, or stdlib.

pythonCodeLensLite.references.filterImpo.description: Exclude occurrences located inside 'import' or 'from' statements from the total count and peek view.

pythonCodeLensLite.references.filterDefi.description: Exclude the underlying definition's location from the reference count.

pythonCodeLensLite.references.filterSelf.description: Exclude references located inside the definition's own scope.

pythonCodeLensLite.references.minCount.description: Hide the lens if the count (after filtering) is less than this value.

pythonCodeLensLite.references.showZero.description: If false, lenses corresponding to exactly zero references will be hidden.

pythonCodeLensLite.lenses.showImplementa.description: Show an additional 'N implementations' lens specifically for Python classes.

pythonCodeLensLite.clickAction.description: Behavior when clicking the CodeLens. 'peek' opens an inline peek view; 'reveal' opens the references panel.

pythonCodeLensLite.performance.debounceM.description: Delay in milliseconds to wait before updating CodeLenses after typing.

pythonCodeLensLite.performance.maxFileSi.description: Skip emitting CodeLens in Python files exceeding this size (in KB).

pythonCodeLensLite.exclude.description: Glob patterns indicating files where CodeLens should skip calculation.
